title: Defending American Democracy from Cyber Threats
summary: Former Pentagon “cyber czar” Eric Rosenbach is Co-Director of Harvard Kennedy School’s Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and head of the new Defending Digital Democracy project. Rosenbach outlines the various cyber threats against American election systems and democratic processes – and how to better protect our democracy from nation-state attacks.
body: Former Pentagon “cyber czar” Eric Rosenbach is Co-Director of Harvard Kennedy School’s Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and head of the new Defending Digital Democracy project. Rosenbach outlines the various cyber threats against American election systems and democratic processes – and how to better protect our democracy from nation-state attacks. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article discusses the cyber threats facing American democracy, as outlined by Eric Rosenbach, Co-Director of the Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs and head of the Defending Digital Democracy project. It focuses on the vulnerabilities of U.S. election systems and democratic processes to nation-state cyber attacks. The piece references the project's goal of developing strategies and resources to better protect these critical infrastructures. It includes links to related resources on managing election technology, security preparedness, and contingency planning, emphasizing a practical approach to defense.
result.main_content_zh: 本文以哈佛大学贝尔弗中心联合主任兼“捍卫数字民主”项目负责人埃里克·罗森巴赫的视角，阐述了美国民主制度面临的网络威胁。文章重点分析了美国选举系统和民主进程在面对民族国家网络攻击时的脆弱性，并介绍了该项目旨在制定策略、提供资源以加强保护的相关工作。文中引用了关于选举技术管理、安全准备和应急计划的相关资源链接，强调了采取切实可行的防御措施来保障民主制度的重要性。
